question: Has UCB1 empirical-yield dispatch (wired S391) improved domain allocation quality compared to the heuristic era?
expect: Domain dispatch Gini improved from 0.468 to <0.40. STRUGGLING domains (governance, economy, brain) dispatched >=1 time each. UCB1 explore term rotated domains more evenly than heuristic scoring.
method: Parsed SWARM-LANES.md + SWARM-LANES-ARCHIVE.md for all DOMEX lanes. Computed dispatch counts per domain abbreviation for S391-S415 (post-UCB1) and S370-S390 (pre-UCB1 comparable window). Calculated Gini coefficients, merge rates, META concentration, and domain rotation per 5-session window. Used dispatch_optimizer.py --all --json for current UCB1 scores.

f_exp1_baseline.note: L-750 reported 0.55 heuristic vs 0.42 UCB1 at S385

score_gini.note: Score Gini is low (good evenness in scoring) but dispatch Gini is higher (scoring does not equal behavior, L-671)

merge_rates.note: Merge rate improvement is real but confounded by EAD enforcement (S331), mode field (S393), and colony effects

governance.verdict: ZERO dispatches post-UCB1. UCB1 did NOT rescue this domain

economy.verdict: Dispatched 3x, merge rate DECLINED. Still MIXED (0.71 outcome rate, n=14)

brain.verdict: Dispatched 5x with 100% merge. Improved from STRUGGLING to MIXED (0.73 rate, n=22)

meta_concentration.note: META share nearly doubled. UCB1 exploit rewards META's high merge rate (0.77) and large lesson pool

domain_rotation_per_5session_window.note: Rotation collapsed from 21 to 7 domains in last 10 sessions. UCB1 explore term insufficient at high N

ucb1_mechanism_analysis.finding: At total_dispatches=120, explore term for META (n=21) = 0.33, for economy (n=3) = 0.97. Spread is only 0.64 points — insufficient to overcome exploit gap of 1.7 (META 4.0 vs ECO 2.3). Explore term is logarithmic and compresses at high N.
ucb1_mechanism_analysis.explore_fraction_top10: exploit dominates: mean exploit=2.67 vs mean explore=0.87 (3.1:1 ratio)

actual: Dispatch Gini WORSENED: 0.431 -> 0.473 (+0.042). Governance got ZERO dispatches. META concentration rose from 11% to 18%. Merge rate improved +18.2pp but confounded. UCB1 score Gini is low (0.134) but score-to-behavior gap persists (L-671). Rotation collapsed to 7 domains in last 10 sessions.
diff: Expected Gini improvement (<0.40): WRONG (0.473). Expected all STRUGGLING domains dispatched: WRONG (governance=0). Expected UCB1 explore rotates evenly: PARTIALLY WRONG (rotation collapsed in recent windows). Brain improved to 100% merge: CORRECT. Merge rate +18.2pp: real but confounded.
verdict: PARTIALLY FALSIFIED. UCB1 scoring produces even scores (Gini 0.134) but uneven dispatches (Gini 0.473). Three failure modes: (1) exploit term rewards META's large history, concentrating dispatches; (2) explore term logarithmically compresses at high N, losing rotation power; (3) score evenness does NOT produce behavior evenness (reconfirms L-671). Brain improved; governance starved. Net: UCB1 is better than R²=-0.089 structural weights (merge rate +18pp) but worse at rotation than the heuristic era (Gini +0.04).
key_insight: UCB1 solves the WHAT-to-dispatch problem (exploit: which domains produce value?) but worsens the WHERE-to-dispatch problem (explore: which domains are underserved?). The explore term sqrt(log(N)/n) compresses at high N, making exploitation dominant. META's structural advantage (high merge rate + large lesson pool) compounds under UCB1's multiplicative exploit formula (merge_rate * (1 + log(lessons))). Score-to-behavior gap (L-671) means that even correctly scored low-Gini recommendations do not produce low-Gini actual dispatches. The fundamental tension: UCB1 optimizes for expected yield, but the swarm also needs diversity — these objectives conflict when one domain persistently outperforms.
